# Work Order & Incident Report — DOC1162
**Document type:** Work Order / Incident Narrative (synthetic narrative record)
**Failure:** F1102 — Power Failure
**Equipment:** EQ-CR-03 — Overhead Crane (Rolling Mill Bay) #3 (Rajendra Steel Plant, Mumbai Unit - Rolling Mill Bay)
**Event timestamp:** 2025-09-30 17:38:04  |  **RCA:** RCA1102 (closed 2025-10-05)

## 1. Incident Narrative
At 17:38:04 on 2025-09-30, the Overhead Crane (Rolling Mill Bay) #3 raised a power failure alarm and was taken out of normal operation. Condition monitoring captured air temperature 301.4 K, process temperature 310.1 K, rotational speed 2389 rpm, torque 17.6 Nm and accumulated tool wear 69 minutes at the moment of the trip. The shift crew isolated the equipment under the plant permit-to-work system and raised this work order for investigation and repair.

## 2. Response & Diagnosis
Naveen Malhotra (Shift C), Swati Desai (Shift A) attended the equipment and completed the make-safe checks before handover to the investigation team. The root cause analysis RCA1102, performed by Arjun Menon (quality inspector, Shift B), concluded: Root cause was a power-delivery failure: the product of rotational speed and torque pushed process power outside the acceptable window, indicating a drive/supply anomaly. An unstable supply and drive fault were the primary contributors.

## 3. Procedure Review
The investigation reviewed the governing procedure PROC-014 for this asset and recorded a compliance finding: Step 2 of PROC-014 ("Visually inspect refractory lining for cracking, spalling and wear, measuring residual thickness at marked reference points.") — the electrical isolation/calibration check was missed in the prior PM cycle. This finding is the actionable link between the maintenance routine and the failure mechanism, and it was logged so recurrence checks can compare like-for-like across the fleet.

## 4. Corrective Work Completed
Inspected and repaired the drive, verified supply stability, and added power-envelope monitoring with an automatic trip. Parts and consumables used were booked to this work order, and post-repair verification confirmed the equipment returned to its qualified operating envelope before release back to production.

## 5. Closure
Work order closed on 2025-10-05 with the corrective action verified in service. This record is a synthetic narrative generated for the Synapse knowledge graph and is tagged accordingly; it mirrors the structure of plant work-order documentation for retrieval purposes. Linked records: failure F1102, RCA RCA1102, procedure PROC-014, equipment EQ-CR-03.
